title.rendered: Graphic Designers for Hire for the Nonprofit Sector

content.rendered:
As the shift to digital marketing and fundraising continues to rapidly increase in the nonprofit sector, it is inevitable that your nonprofit will need to invest in professional graphic design. Below is a list of recommended graphic designers that have experience working with nonprofits, NGOs, and charities. For your reference:

⭐ = $50 USD/hour
⭐⭐ = $100 USD/hour
⭐⭐⭐ = $150 USD/hour

For a price quote, please reach out to the designers directly through their website.

Andrea Gonzalez :: plantandoideas.com
Mexico City, Mexico
Hourly Rate: ⭐⭐

Angie Violino :: atelierdoodle.com
Milan, Italy
Hourly Rate: ⭐

Beth Singer Design, LLC :: bethsingerdesign.com
Arlington, VA
Hourly Rate: ⭐⭐⭐

Christine Urbanek :: creativehotlist.com/curbanek
Murphys, CA
Hourly Rate: ⭐⭐

Jill Krieger :: jillkrieger.co
Denver, CO
Hourly Rate: ⭐⭐

Lisa Kaats :: lisakaats.com
San Diego, CA
Hourly Rate: ⭐

Obsesso Processo :: obsessoprocesso.com
Denver, CO
Hourly Rate: ⭐⭐⭐

Olivia Wheeler :: actoncircle.co
San Francisco, CA
Hourly Rate: ⭐⭐⭐

Stacey Uy :: staceyuy.com
San Diego, CA
Hourly Rate:⭐⭐

Have a graphic designer outside of the United States recommend? Please contact Heather Mansfield with their name, city/country, a link to the portfolio/website, and their hourly rate. To be listed, the designer must have extensive experience working with the nonprofit sector which must be reflected in their online portfolio. The goal is to build an international list of designers to ensure a diversity in language.

101 Digital Marketing & Fundraising Best Practices for Nonprofits is a blog and webinar series (written and presented by Heather Mansfield) on website design, email marketing, online fundraising, and social media best practices for nonprofits, NGOs, and charities worldwide. Those who register and attend all three webinars in the series will earn a Certificate in Digital Marketing & Fundraising from Nonprofit Tech for Good.
